summary: Cleaned up item-related types by moving character-item variable structs from common item headers into itCharItems.h, replacing a locally defined ECB with a shared lb/types.h itECB type, and tightening several item variable uses in freeze, whitebear, nokonoko, it_2725, it_2E5A, and Dr. Mario pill code. The PR mattered because it reduced duplicated/ambiguous item type definitions, improved pointer typing for freeze-linked item state, and made item ECB usage more explicit while preserving decomp matching constraints.
what_changed: Removed the local struct ECB definition from src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h and deleted its forward declaration from src/melee/it/forward.h.
Added typedef struct _itECB in src/melee/lb/types.h with four f32 fields: top, bottom, right, and left.

Updated item structures and APIs to use itECB instead of ECB, including ItemAttr fields x20 and x40, Item fields xBDC through xC1C, HSD_ObjAllocUnk2::ecb_arr, it_80275D5C's prototype, and stack locals in it_2725.c and itnokonoko.c.

Moved several character-specific item variable and attribute structs out of itCommonItems.h into itCharItems.h: itMasterHandLaser_ItemVars, itPeachToadSporeAttributes, itPeachTurnipAttributes, itPeachTurnip_ItemVars, itPikachutJoltGround_ItemVars, and itSamusGrapple_ItemVars.
Expanded itFreeze_ItemVars so unk_1C is typed as Item_GObj* instead of int and added x20 as UNK_T.
Updated itfreeze.c to use xDD4_itemVar.freeze.unk_1C instead of borrowing the foxblaster xDF0 field for the linked item pointer, and reset that pointer to NULL.
Updated itwhitebea.c so it_802E37A4 clears GET_ITEM(gobj)->xDD4_itemVar.freeze.x20 rather than whitebea.x20, with an explanatory comment that the function is only called by it_8028F2B4 from itfreeze.
Adjusted it_2E5A.c casts and itCommonItems.h data attributes from ECB* to itECB*.

Made small Dr. Mario pill cleanup/matching edits: included dolphin/types.h for M_PI_2, renamed local HSD_JObj variable from hobj to jobj, avoided GET_JOBJ because it changed stack behavior, used bool comparisons against true, used GET_ITEM in one callback, and renamed inline helper parameters/locals to reduce GObj/HObj confusion.
smart_moves: Introduced itECB in lb/types.h rather than keeping an item-local ECB, making the collision-box-like type reusable while avoiding a generic name that could conflict with fighter ECB concepts.
Renamed ECB to itECB everywhere touched, which documents that this is the item-specific four-float box layout, distinct from ftECB in lb/types.h.
Moved character-item structs into itCharItems.h, reducing itCommonItems.h clutter and grouping type ownership by item category.
Replaced freeze code's access through xDD4_itemVar.foxblaster.xDF0 with xDD4_itemVar.freeze.unk_1C, eliminating an obviously wrong union arm even though exact semantic names remain unknown.
Kept decomp matching in mind in itdrmariopill.c by explicitly noting that GET_JOBJ changed stack layout and preserving gobj->hsd_obj access instead.
Used NULL for item object pointers after typing freeze.unk_1C as Item_GObj*, improving pointer intent without overnaming unknown fields.

Reviewer and author explicitly recorded uncertainty about whether it_802E37A4 should use freeze or whitebear item vars, choosing a cautious current best guess and leaving room to revisit after more whitebear decompilation.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave unrelated item union arms in use just because they match layout; if evidence shows an item uses its own item var region, type that field under the correct item var struct even if the field name remains unk_*.
When two subsystems have similarly named concepts, prefer subsystem-prefixed type names such as itECB and ftECB to prevent ambiguity and accidental header coupling.
Header cleanup can be a matching aid: moving structs to the header that owns their item category reduces accidental dependencies and makes future variable identification easier.
Avoid convenience macros like GET_JOBJ if they perturb stack layout in a matching function; a direct field access may be necessary and should be commented when used for matching reasons.

For partially decompiled item interactions, especially linked items, distinguish caller item vars from callee item vars carefully. The linked GObj may not be the same item kind as the function's file location suggests.

When evidence is incomplete because spawn callbacks or related item files are not yet decompiled, keep field names conservative and document the assumption rather than assigning strong semantic names.
naming_conventions: Item-specific ECB layout was named itECB, while fighter ECB remains ftECB.

Character item variable structs use item-name-prefixed CamelCase plus _ItemVars, such as itMasterHandLaser_ItemVars, itPeachTurnip_ItemVars, itPikachutJoltGround_ItemVars, and itSamusGrapple_ItemVars.
Character item attribute structs use item-name-prefixed CamelCase plus Attributes, such as itPeachToadSporeAttributes and itPeachTurnipAttributes.
Unknown fields retained offset-based names such as xDD4, xDD8, xDDC_damage, xDE0_scl, xDE4_owner, xDE8_scl, unk_1C, and x20 rather than inventing names.
Pointer fields were converted to pointer-appropriate types and NULL assignment where possible, for example itFreeze_ItemVars::unk_1C as Item_GObj*.
Local variable names in Dr. Mario pill code were adjusted to avoid misleading names: HSD_JObj* jobj for joint objects, arg_gobj for input GObj, and gobj for a retrieved linked GObj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: In itdrmariopill.c, a comment states that using GET_JOBJ(gobj) messes up the stack, so the code keeps HSD_JObj* jobj = gobj->hsd_obj for matching.
Replaced a literal 1.5707963267948966 rotation constant with M_PI_2 after adding dolphin/types.h, likely preserving intent while using the project math constant.
Changed comparisons such as ftCo_800BF228(gobj) == 1 and ftDr_Init_80149844(...) == 1 to == true, clarifying boolean-return usage without changing control flow.
Used explicit casts to itECB* in it_2E5A.c where the code still relies on interpreting attribute memory as an item ECB layout.
Retained unknown placeholder types such as UNK_T, UNK_RET, and UNK_PARAMS where exact function or data shapes were not proven.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Questioned whether it_802E37A4 should clear whitebear item vars instead of freeze item vars because the freeze function calls it on a linked item rather than necessarily on the freeze item itself.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.comment: Acknowledged the linked item could be the bear and noted uncertainty because the spawn callback was not decompiled; also observed the whitebear file appears to contain functions for other items such as Ottosea.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.comment: Maintained that freeze item vars were probably more likely for this function based on it_8028F2B4 being the picked_up callback for itfreeze.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Accepted keeping freeze item vars for now, with the caveat that it can be revisited after more whitebear decompilation.

searchable_terms: Item xBDC xBEC xBFC xC0C xC1C
follow_up_queries: When whitebear or freeze spawn callbacks are decompiled, verify whether it_802E37A4 receives a freeze item, whitebear item, or another linked item type.
Audit remaining uses of generic ECB or local ECB definitions and convert to itECB or another subsystem-specific type where appropriate.
Review itwhitebea.c for functions that may actually belong to other item files such as Ottosea, as noted in the PR discussion.
Search for item var union accesses that use unrelated arms like foxblaster fields outside their item context.
Once semantics are clearer, rename itFreeze_ItemVars::unk_1C and x20 from placeholder names to meaningful linked-item state names.
